   336â†’// Visitor allows visiting a Node and its child nodes. The Visit method is
   337â†’// invoked for each node with the path leading to the node provided additionally.
   338â†’// If the result visitor w is not nil and no error, Walk visits each of the children
   339â†’// of node with the visitor w, followed by a call of w.Visit(nil, nil).
   340â†’type Visitor interface {
   341â†’	Visit(node Node, path []Node) (w Visitor, err error)
   342â†’}
   343â†’
   344â†’// Walk traverses an AST in depth-first order: It starts by calling
   345â†’// v.Visit(node, path); node must not be nil. If the visitor w returned by
   346â†’// v.Visit(node, path) is not nil and the visitor returns no error, Walk is
   347â†’// invoked recursively with visitor w for each of the non-nil children of node,
   348â†’// followed by a call of w.Visit(nil), returning an error
   349â†’// As the tree is descended the path of previous nodes is provided.
   350â†’func Walk(v Visitor, node Node, path []Node) error {
   351â†’	var err error
   352â†’	if v, err = v.Visit(node, path); v == nil || err != nil {
   353â†’		return err
   354â†’	}
   355â†’	var pathToHere []Node // Initialized only when needed.
   356â†’
   357â†’	for e := range ChildrenIter(node) {
   358â†’		if pathToHere == nil {
   359â†’			pathToHere = append(path, node)
   360â†’		}
   361â†’		if err := Walk(v, e, pathToHere); err != nil {
   362â†’			return err
   363â†’		}
   364â†’	}
   365â†’
   366â†’	_, err = v.Visit(nil, nil)
   367â†’	return err
   368â†’}

   370â†’func ExtractSelectors(expr Expr) [][]*labels.Matcher {
   371â†’	var selectors [][]*labels.Matcher
   372â†’	Inspect(expr, func(node Node, _ []Node) error {
   373â†’		vs, ok := node.(*VectorSelector)
   374â†’		if ok {
   375â†’			selectors = append(selectors, vs.LabelMatchers)
   376â†’		}
   377â†’		return nil
   378â†’	})
   379â†’	return selectors
   380â†’}

   382â†’type inspector func(Node, []Node) error
   383â†’
   384â†’func (f inspector) Visit(node Node, path []Node) (Visitor, error) {
   385â†’	if err := f(node, path); err != nil {
   386â†’		return nil, err
   387â†’	}
   388â†’
   389â†’	return f, nil
   390â†’}
   391â†’
   392â†’// Inspect traverses an AST in depth-first order: It starts by calling
   393â†’// f(node, path); node must not be nil. If f returns a nil error, Inspect invokes f
   394â†’// for all the non-nil children of node, recursively.
   395â†’// Note: path may be overwritten after f returns; copy path if you need to retain it.
   396â†’func Inspect(node Node, f inspector) {
   397â†’	var pathBuf [4]Node        // To reduce allocations during recursion.
   398â†’	Walk(f, node, pathBuf[:0]) //nolint:errcheck
   399â†’}

   401â†’// ChildrenIter returns an iterator over all child nodes of a syntax tree node.
   402â†’func ChildrenIter(node Node) func(func(Node) bool) {
   403â†’	return func(yield func(Node) bool) {
   404â†’		// According to lore, these switches have significantly better performance than interfaces
   405â†’		switch n := node.(type) {
   406â†’		case *EvalStmt:
   407â†’			yield(n.Expr)
   408â†’		case Expressions:
   409â†’			for _, e := range n {
   410â†’				if !yield(e) {
   411â†’					return
   412â†’				}
   413â†’			}
   414â†’		case *AggregateExpr:
   415â†’			if n.Expr != nil {
   416â†’				if !yield(n.Expr) {
   417â†’					return
   418â†’				}
   419â†’			}
   420â†’			if n.Param != nil {
   421â†’				yield(n.Param)
   422â†’			}
   423â†’		case *BinaryExpr:
   424â†’			if !yield(n.LHS) {
   425â†’				return
   426â†’			}
   427â†’			yield(n.RHS)
   428â†’		case *Call:
   429â†’			for _, e := range n.Args {
   430â†’				if !yield(e) {
   431â†’					return
   432â†’				}
   433â†’			}
   434â†’		case *SubqueryExpr:
   435â†’			yield(n.Expr)
   436â†’		case *ParenExpr:
   437â†’			yield(n.Expr)
   438â†’		case *UnaryExpr:
   439â†’			yield(n.Expr)
   440â†’		case *MatrixSelector:
   441â†’			yield(n.VectorSelector)
   442â†’		case *StepInvariantExpr:
   443â†’			yield(n.Expr)
   444â†’		case *NumberLiteral, *StringLiteral, *VectorSelector:
   445â†’			// nothing to do
   446â†’		default:
   447â†’			panic(fmt.Errorf("promql.ChildrenIter: unhandled node type %T", node))
   448â†’		}
   449â†’	}
   450â†’}
   451â†’
   452â†’// Children returns a list of all child nodes of a syntax tree node.
   453â†’// Implemented for backwards-compatibility; prefer ChildrenIter().
   454â†’func Children(node Node) []Node {
   455â†’	ret := []Node{}
   456â†’	for e := range ChildrenIter(node) {
   457â†’		ret = append(ret, e)
   458â†’	}
   459â†’	return ret
   460â†’}

    40â†’//
    41â†’// vals is a list of the evaluated arguments for the function call.
    42â†’//

    44â†’// Vector, scalars a Vector with one series whose value is the scalar
    45â†’// value,and nil for strings.
    46â†’//
    47â†’// args are the original arguments to the function, where you can access
    48â†’// matrixSelectors, vectorSelectors, and StringLiterals.

    50â†’// enh.Out is a pre-allocated empty vector that you may use to accumulate
    51â†’// output before returning it. The vectors in vals should not be returned.a
    52â†’//
    53â†’// Range vector functions need only return a vector with the right value,
    54â†’// the metric and timestamp are not needed.
    55â†’//
    56â†’// Instant vector functions need only return a vector with the right values and
    57â†’// metrics, the timestamp are not needed.

    59â†’// Scalar results should be returned as the value of a sample in a Vector.

    70â†’// If interpolation is needed (when smoothed is true and the first sample is before the range start),
    71â†’// it returns the interpolated value at the left boundary; otherwise, it returns the first sample's value.
    72â†’func pickOrInterpolateLeft(floats []FPoint, first int, rangeStart int64, smoothed, isCounter bool) float64 {

    79â†’// pickOrInterpolateRight returns the value at the right boundary of the range.

    82â†’func pickOrInterpolateRight(floats []FPoint, last int, rangeEnd int64, smoothed, isCounter bool) float64 {

    89â†’// interpolate performs linear interpolation between two points.
    90â†’// If isCounter is true and there is a counter reset, it models the counter
    91â†’// as starting from 0 (post-reset) by setting y1 to 0.
    92â†’// It then calculates the interpolated value at the given timestamp.
    93â†’func interpolate(p1, p2 FPoint, t int64, isCounter bool) float64 {
    94â†’	y1 := p1.F

    96â†’	if isCounter && y2 < y1 {
    97â†’		y1 = 0
    98â†’	}
    99â†’
   100â†’	return y1 + (y2-y1)*float64(t-p1.T)/float64(p2.T-p1.T)

   158â†’// Run receives and saves target set updates and triggers the scraping loops reloading.
   159â†’// Reloading happens in the background so that it doesn't block receiving targets updates.
   160â†’func (m *Manager) Run(tsets <-chan map[string][]*targetgroup.Group) error {
   161â†’	go m.reloader()
   162â†’	for {
   163â†’		select {
   164â†’		case ts, ok := <-tsets:
   165â†’			if !ok {
   166â†’				break
   167â†’			}
   168â†’			m.updateTsets(ts)
   169â†’
   170â†’			select {
   171â†’			case m.triggerReload <- struct{}{}:
   172â†’			default:
   173â†’			}
   174â†’
   175â†’		case <-m.graceShut:
   176â†’			return nil
   177â†’		}
   178â†’	}
   179â†’}

   186â†’func (m *Manager) reloader() {
   187â†’	reloadIntervalDuration := m.opts.DiscoveryReloadInterval
   188â†’	if reloadIntervalDuration == model.Duration(0) {
   189â†’		reloadIntervalDuration = model.Duration(5 * time.Second)
   190â†’	}
   191â†’
   192â†’	ticker := time.NewTicker(time.Duration(reloadIntervalDuration))
   193â†’
   194â†’	defer ticker.Stop()
   195â†’
   196â†’	for {
   197â†’		select {
   198â†’		case <-m.graceShut:
   199â†’			return
   200â†’		case <-ticker.C:
   201â†’			select {
   202â†’			case <-m.triggerReload:
   203â†’				m.reload()
   204â†’			case <-m.graceShut:
   205â†’				return
   206â†’			}
   207â†’		}
   208â†’	}
   209â†’}

   211â†’func (m *Manager) reload() {
   212â†’	m.mtxScrape.Lock()
   213â†’	var wg sync.WaitGroup
   214â†’	for setName, groups := range m.targetSets {
   215â†’		if _, ok := m.scrapePools[setName]; !ok {
   216â†’			scrapeConfig, ok := m.scrapeConfigs[setName]
   217â†’			if !ok {
   218â†’				m.logger.Error("error reloading target set", "err", "invalid config id:"+setName)
   219â†’				continue
   220â†’			}
   221â†’			m.metrics.targetScrapePools.Inc()
   222â†’			sp, err := newScrapePool(scrapeConfig, m.appendable, m.appendableV2, m.offsetSeed, m.logger.With("scrape_pool", setName), m.buffers, m.opts, m.metrics)
   223â†’			if err != nil {
   224â†’				m.metrics.targetScrapePoolsFailed.Inc()
   225â†’				m.logger.Error("error creating new scrape pool", "err", err, "scrape_pool", setName)
   226â†’				continue
   227â†’			}
   228â†’			m.scrapePools[setName] = sp
   229â†’			if l, ok := m.scrapeFailureLoggers[scrapeConfig.ScrapeFailureLogFile]; ok {
   230â†’				sp.SetScrapeFailureLogger(l)
   231â†’			} else {
   232â†’				sp.logger.Error("No logger found. This is a bug in Prometheus that should be reported upstream.", "scrape_pool", setName)
   233â†’			}
   234â†’		}
   235â†’
   236â†’		wg.Add(1)
   237â†’		// Run the sync in parallel as these take a while and at high load can't catch up.
   238â†’		go func(sp *scrapePool, groups []*targetgroup.Group) {
   239â†’			sp.Sync(groups)
   240â†’			wg.Done()
   241â†’		}(m.scrapePools[setName], groups)
   242â†’	}
   243â†’	m.mtxScrape.Unlock()
   244â†’	wg.Wait()
   245â†’}

   278â†’// ApplyConfig resets the manager's target providers and job configurations as defined by the new cfg.
   279â†’func (m *Manager) ApplyConfig(cfg *config.Config) error {
   280â†’	m.mtxScrape.Lock()
   281â†’	defer m.mtxScrape.Unlock()
   282â†’
   283â†’	scfgs, err := cfg.GetScrapeConfigs()
   284â†’	if err != nil {
   285â†’		return err
   286â†’	}
   287â†’
   288â†’	c := make(map[string]*config.ScrapeConfig)
   289â†’	scrapeFailureLoggers := map[string]FailureLogger{
   290â†’		"": nil, // Emptying the file name sets the scrape logger to nil.
   291â†’	}
   292â†’	for _, scfg := range scfgs {
   293â†’		c[scfg.JobName] = scfg
   294â†’		if _, ok := scrapeFailureLoggers[scfg.ScrapeFailureLogFile]; !ok {
   295â†’			// We promise to reopen the file on each reload.
   296â†’			var (
   297â†’				logger FailureLogger
   298â†’				err    error
   299â†’			)
   300â†’			if m.newScrapeFailureLogger != nil {
   301â†’				if logger, err = m.newScrapeFailureLogger(scfg.ScrapeFailureLogFile); err != nil {
   302â†’					return err
   303â†’				}
   304â†’			}
   305â†’			scrapeFailureLoggers[scfg.ScrapeFailureLogFile] = logger
   306â†’		}
   307â†’	}
   308â†’	m.scrapeConfigs = c
   309â†’
   310â†’	oldScrapeFailureLoggers := m.scrapeFailureLoggers
   311â†’	for _, s := range oldScrapeFailureLoggers {
   312â†’		if s != nil {
   313â†’			defer s.Close()
   314â†’		}
   315â†’	}
   316â†’
   317â†’	m.scrapeFailureLoggers = scrapeFailureLoggers
   318â†’
   319â†’	if err := m.setOffsetSeed(cfg.GlobalConfig.ExternalLabels); err != nil {
   320â†’		return err
   321â†’	}
   322â†’
   323â†’	// Cleanup and reload pool if the configuration has changed.
   324â†’	var (
   325â†’		failed   atomic.Bool
   326â†’		wg       sync.WaitGroup
   327â†’		toDelete sync.Map // Stores the list of names of pools to delete.
   328â†’	)
   329â†’
   330â†’	// Use a buffered channel to limit reload concurrency.
   331â†’	// Each scrape pool writes the channel before we start to reload it and read from it at the end.
   332â†’	// This means only N pools can be reloaded at the same time.
   333â†’	canReload := make(chan int, runtime.GOMAXPROCS(0))
   334â†’	for poolName, pool := range m.scrapePools {
   335â†’		canReload <- 1
   336â†’		wg.Add(1)
   337â†’		cfg, ok := m.scrapeConfigs[poolName]
   338â†’		// Reload each scrape pool in a dedicated goroutine so we don't have to wait a long time
   339â†’		// if we have a lot of scrape pools to update.
   340â†’		go func(name string, sp *scrapePool, cfg *config.ScrapeConfig, ok bool) {
   341â†’			defer func() {
   342â†’				wg.Done()
   343â†’				<-canReload
   344â†’			}()
   345â†’			switch {
   346â†’			case !ok:
   347â†’				sp.stop()
   348â†’				toDelete.Store(name, struct{}{})
   349â†’			case !reflect.DeepEqual(sp.config, cfg):
   350â†’				err := sp.reload(cfg)
   351â†’				if err != nil {
   352â†’					m.logger.Error("error reloading scrape pool", "err", err, "scrape_pool", name)
   353â†’					failed.Store(true)
   354â†’				}
   355â†’				fallthrough
   356â†’			case ok:
   357â†’				if l, ok := m.scrapeFailureLoggers[cfg.ScrapeFailureLogFile]; ok {
   358â†’					sp.SetScrapeFailureLogger(l)
   359â†’				} else {
   360â†’					sp.logger.Error("No logger found. This is a bug in Prometheus that should be reported upstream.", "scrape_pool", name)
   361â†’				}
   362â†’			}
   363â†’		}(poolName, pool, cfg, ok)
   364â†’	}
   365â†’	wg.Wait()
   366â†’
   367â†’	toDelete.Range(func(name, _ any) bool {
   368â†’		delete(m.scrapePools, name.(string))
   369â†’		return true
   370â†’	})
   371â†’
   372â†’	if failed.Load() {
   373â†’		return errors.New("failed to apply the new configuration")
   374â†’	}
   375â†’	return nil
   376â†’}

   449â†’// DisableEndOfRunStalenessMarkers disables the end-of-run staleness markers for the provided targets in the given
   450â†’// targetSet. When the end-of-run staleness is disabled for a target, when it goes away, there will be no staleness
   451â†’// markers written for its series.
   452â†’func (m *Manager) DisableEndOfRunStalenessMarkers(targetSet string, targets []*Target) {
   453â†’	// This avoids mutex lock contention.
   454â†’	if len(targets) == 0 {
   455â†’		return
   456â†’	}
   457â†’
   458â†’	// Only hold the lock to find the scrape pool
   459â†’	m.mtxScrape.Lock()
   460â†’	sp, ok := m.scrapePools[targetSet]
   461â†’	m.mtxScrape.Unlock()
   462â†’
   463â†’	if ok {
   464â†’		sp.disableEndOfRunStalenessMarkers(targets)
   465â†’	}
   466â†’}

    37â†’// A RecordingRule records its vector expression into new timeseries.
    38â†’type RecordingRule struct {
    39â†’	name   string
    40â†’	vector parser.Expr
    41â†’	labels labels.Labels
    42â†’	// The health of the recording rule.
    43â†’	health *atomic.String
    44â†’	// Timestamp of last evaluation of the recording rule.
    45â†’	evaluationTimestamp *atomic.Time
    46â†’	// The last error seen by the recording rule.
    47â†’	lastError *atomic.Error
    48â†’	// Duration of how long it took to evaluate the recording rule.
    49â†’	evaluationDuration *atomic.Duration
    50â†’
    51â†’	dependenciesMutex sync.RWMutex
    52â†’	dependentRules    []Rule
    53â†’	dependencyRules   []Rule
    54â†’}

    56â†’// NewRecordingRule returns a new recording rule.
    57â†’func NewRecordingRule(name string, vector parser.Expr, lset labels.Labels) *RecordingRule {
    58â†’	return &RecordingRule{
    59â†’		name:                name,
    60â†’		vector:              vector,
    61â†’		labels:              lset,
    62â†’		health:              atomic.NewString(string(HealthUnknown)),
    63â†’		evaluationTimestamp: atomic.NewTime(time.Time{}),
    64â†’		evaluationDuration:  atomic.NewDuration(0),
    65â†’		lastError:           atomic.NewError(nil),
    66â†’	}
    67â†’}

    84â†’// Eval evaluates the rule and then overrides the metric names and labels accordingly.
    85â†’func (rule *RecordingRule) Eval(ctx context.Context, queryOffset time.Duration, ts time.Time, query QueryFunc, _ *url.URL, limit int) (promql.Vector, error) {
    86â†’	ctx = NewOriginContext(ctx, NewRuleDetail(rule))
    87â†’	vector, err := query(ctx, rule.vector.String(), ts.Add(-queryOffset))
    88â†’	if err != nil {
    89â†’		return nil, err
    90â†’	}
    91â†’
    92â†’	// Override the metric name and labels.
    93â†’	lb := labels.NewBuilder(labels.EmptyLabels())
    94â†’
    95â†’	for i := range vector {
    96â†’		sample := &vector[i]
    97â†’
    98â†’		lb.Reset(sample.Metric)
    99â†’		lb.Set(labels.MetricName, rule.name)
   100â†’
   101â†’		rule.labels.Range(func(l labels.Label) {
   102â†’			lb.Set(l.Name, l.Value)
   103â†’		})
   104â†’
   105â†’		sample.Metric = lb.Labels()
   106â†’	}
   107â†’
   108â†’	// Check that the rule does not produce identical metrics after applying
   109â†’	// labels.
   110â†’	if vector.ContainsSameLabelset() {
   111â†’		return nil, ErrDuplicateRecordingLabelSet
   112â†’	}
   113â†’
   114â†’	numSeries := len(vector)
   115â†’	if limit > 0 && numSeries > limit {
   116â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("exceeded limit of %d with %d series", limit, numSeries)
   117â†’	}
   118â†’
   119â†’	rule.SetHealth(HealthGood)
   120â†’	rule.SetLastError(err)
   121â†’	return vector, nil
   122â†’}
   123â†’
   124â†’func (rule *RecordingRule) String() string {
   125â†’	r := rulefmt.Rule{
   126â†’		Record: rule.name,
   127â†’		Expr:   rule.vector.String(),
   128â†’		Labels: rule.labels.Map(),
   129â†’	}
   130â†’
   131â†’	byt, err := yaml.Marshal(r)
   132â†’	if err != nil {
   133â†’		return fmt.Sprintf("error marshaling recording rule: %q", err.Error())
   134â†’	}
   135â†’
   136â†’	return string(byt)
   137â†’}

   179â†’func (rule *RecordingRule) SetDependentRules(dependents []Rule) {
   180â†’	rule.dependenciesMutex.Lock()
   181â†’	defer rule.dependenciesMutex.Unlock()
   182â†’
   183â†’	rule.dependentRules = make([]Rule, len(dependents))
   184â†’	copy(rule.dependentRules, dependents)
   185â†’}
   186â†’
   187â†’func (rule *RecordingRule) NoDependentRules() bool {
   188â†’	rule.dependenciesMutex.RLock()
   189â†’	defer rule.dependenciesMutex.RUnlock()
   190â†’
   191â†’	if rule.dependentRules == nil {
   192â†’		return false // We don't know if there are dependent rules.
   193â†’	}
   194â†’
   195â†’	return len(rule.dependentRules) == 0

   203â†’// SelectHints specifies hints passed for data selections.

   206â†’	Start int64 // Start time in milliseconds for this select.

   209â†’	// Maximum number of results returned. Use a value of 0 to disable.

   217â†’	Range    int64    // Range vector selector range in milliseconds.
   218â†’
   219â†’	// ShardCount is the total number of shards that series should be split into
   220â†’	// at query time. Then, only series in the ShardIndex shard will be returned
   221â†’	// by the query.
   222â†’	//
   223â†’	// ShardCount equal to 0 means that sharding is disabled.
   224â†’	ShardCount uint64
   225â†’
   226â†’	// ShardIndex is the series shard index to query. The index must be between 0 and ShardCount-1.
   227â†’	// When ShardCount is set to a value > 0, then a query will only process series within the
   228â†’	// ShardIndex's shard.
   229â†’	//
   230â†’	// Series are sharded by "labels stable hash" mod "ShardCount".
   231â†’	ShardIndex uint64
   232â†’
   233â†’	// DisableTrimming allows to disable trimming of matching series chunks based on query Start and End time.

   235â†’	// may be improved.
   236â†’	DisableTrimming bool
   237â†’
   238â†’	// Projection hints. They are currently unused in the Prometheus promql engine but can be used by different
   239â†’	// implementations of the Queryable interface and engines.
   240â†’	// These hints are useful for queries like `sum by (label) (rate(metric[5m]))` - we can safely evaluate it
   241â†’	// even if we only fetch the `label` label. For some storage implementations this is beneficial.
   242â†’
   243â†’	// ProjectionLabels are the minimum amount of labels required to be fetched for this Select call
   244â†’	// When honored it is required to add an __series_hash__ label containing the hash of all labels
   245â†’	// of a particular series so that the engine can still perform horizontal joins.
   246â†’	ProjectionLabels []string

   248â†’	// ProjectionInclude defines if we have to include or exclude the labels from the ProjectLabels field.

   280â†’// Appender provides batched appends against a storage.
   281â†’// It must be completed with a call to Commit or Rollback and must not be reused afterwards.
   282â†’//
   283â†’// Operations on the Appender interface are not goroutine-safe.
   284â†’//
   285â†’// The order of samples appended via the Appender is preserved within each series.
   286â†’// I.e. timestamp order within batch is not validated, samples are not reordered per timestamp or by float/histogram
   287â†’// type.
   288â†’//
   289â†’// WARNING(bwplotka): Switch to AppendableV2 is in progress (https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/17632).
   290â†’// Appender will be removed soon (ETA: Q2 2026).
   291â†’type Appender interface {
   292â†’	AppenderTransaction
   293â†’
   294â†’	// Append adds a sample pair for the given series.
   295â†’	// An optional series reference can be provided to accelerate calls.
   296â†’	// A series reference number is returned which can be used to add further
   297â†’	// samples to the given series in the same or later transactions.
   298â†’	// Returned reference numbers are ephemeral and may be rejected in calls
   299â†’	// to Append() at any point. Adding the sample via Append() returns a new
   300â†’	// reference number.
   301â†’	// If the reference is 0 it must not be used for caching.
   302â†’	Append(ref SeriesRef, l labels.Labels, t int64, v float64) (SeriesRef, error)
   303â†’
   304â†’	// SetOptions configures the appender with specific append options such as
   305â†’	// discarding out-of-order samples even if out-of-order is enabled in the TSDB.
   306â†’	SetOptions(opts *AppendOptions)
   307â†’
   308â†’	ExemplarAppender
   309â†’	HistogramAppender
   310â†’	MetadataUpdater
   311â†’	StartTimestampAppender
   312â†’}

   314â†’// GetRef is an extra interface on Appenders used by downstream projects
   315â†’// (e.g. Cortex) to avoid maintaining a parallel set of references.
   316â†’type GetRef interface {
   317â†’	// GetRef returns a reference number that can be used to pass to AppenderV2.Append(),
   318â†’	// and a set of labels that will not cause another copy when passed to AppenderV2.Append().
   319â†’	// 0 means the appender does not have a reference to this series.
   320â†’	// hash should be a hash of lset.
   321â†’	GetRef(lset labels.Labels, hash uint64) (SeriesRef, labels.Labels)
   322â†’}

   324â†’// ExemplarAppender provides an interface for adding samples to exemplar storage, which
   325â†’// within Prometheus is in-memory only.
   326â†’//
   327â†’// WARNING(bwplotka): Switch to AppendableV2 is in progress (https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/17632).
   328â†’// ExemplarAppender will be removed soon (ETA: Q2 2026).
   329â†’type ExemplarAppender interface {

   333â†’	// exemplars in the same or later transactions.
   334â†’	// Returned reference numbers are ephemeral and may be rejected in calls
   335â†’	// to Append() at any point. Adding the sample via Append() returns a new
   336â†’	// reference number.
   337â†’	// If the reference is 0 it must not be used for caching.
   338â†’	// Note that in our current implementation of Prometheus' exemplar storage

   344â†’// HistogramAppender provides an interface for appending histograms to the storage.
   345â†’//
   346â†’// WARNING(bwplotka): Switch to AppendableV2 is in progress (https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/issues/17632).
   347â†’// HistogramAppender will be removed soon (ETA: Q2 2026).
   348â†’type HistogramAppender interface {
   349â†’	// AppendHistogram adds a histogram for the given series labels. An
   350â†’	// optional reference number can be provided to accelerate calls. A
   351â†’	// reference number is returned which can be used to add further
   352â†’	// histograms in the same or later transactions. Returned reference
   353â†’	// numbers are ephemeral and may be rejected in calls to Append() at any
   354â†’	// point. Adding the sample via Append() returns a new reference number.
   355â†’	// If the reference is 0, it must not be used for caching.
   356â†’	//
   357â†’	// For efficiency reasons, the histogram is passed as a
   358â†’	// pointer. AppendHistogram won't mutate the histogram, but in turn
   359â†’	// depends on the caller to not mutate it either.

   361â†’	// AppendHistogramSTZeroSample adds synthetic zero sample for the given st timestamp,
   362â†’	// which will be associated with given series, labels and the incoming
   363â†’	// sample's t (timestamp). AppendHistogramSTZeroSample returns error if zero sample can't be

   369â†’	// ST for the given series in the same or later transactions.
   370â†’	// Returned reference numbers are ephemeral and may be rejected in calls
   371â†’	// to AppendHistogramSTZeroSample() at any point.
   372â†’	//
   373â†’	// If the reference is 0 it must not be used for caching.
   374â†’	AppendHistogramSTZeroSample(ref SeriesRef, l labels.Labels, t, st int64, h *histogram.Histogram, fh *histogram.FloatHistogram) (SeriesRef, error)
   375â†’}

   102â†’func (a *Alert) needsSending(ts time.Time, resendDelay time.Duration) bool {
   103â†’	if a.State == StatePending {
   104â†’		return false
   105â†’	}
   106â†’
   107â†’	// if an alert has been resolved since the last send, resend it
   108â†’	if a.ResolvedAt.After(a.LastSentAt) {
   109â†’		return true
   110â†’	}
   111â†’
   112â†’	return a.LastSentAt.Add(resendDelay).Before(ts)
   113â†’}
